channel.title: BBC News
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: PC Harper's widow criticises early prisoner release plan as Burnham to review scheme
item.description: Lissie Harper says the possible early release of prisoners is "deplorable", after reports that two of PC Harper's killers could be freed early.

item.title: Former defence minister Al Carns turns down ministerial offer
item.description: Carns quit following the resignation of John Healey as defence secretary in a row over military funding.

item.title: Most bus fares in England to be capped at £2 from January
item.description: The government says the policy will "help with the cost of living and give people the breathing space they need"

item.title: Defence must cut waste and maximise value for money, says Streeting
item.description: A £15bn increase in military spending announced by the last government is not yet fully funded.

item.title: Natalie Fleet leaves 'triggering' safeguarding minister role
item.description: The MP for Bolsover has spoken about how she was groomed and raped as a teenager.

item.title: Burnham rebukes ministers after prisoner releases joke
item.description: It comes after Wes Streeting apologises over comments caught on camera ahead of Tuesday's cabinet.

item.title: Chris Mason: Sarwar ministerial role sees promotion of another Starmer critic
item.description: Months after calling on Keir Starmer to quit, the Scottish Labour leader is set to become a minister in Burnham's government, the BBC's political editor writes.

item.title: No immediate change to income tax allowance, says Burnham
item.description: The new prime minister previously said voters were frustrated over the freeze in tax-free personal allowance.

item.title: Boost youth jobs by cutting employers' national insurance, MPs urge
item.description: A select committee says it has heard "overwhelming evidence" that rising employment costs are reducing training and job vacancies.

item.title: Scottish Labour leader Anas Sarwar set to become a minister in Burnham's government
item.description: It would mean Sarwar leaving the Scottish Parliament and his leader role, then taking up a seat in the House of Lords.

item.title: Burnham tells cabinet to tackle living costs at first meeting
item.description: The new prime minister chaired his first cabinet after announcing a cut to VAT on household electricity.

item.title: Starmer ally takes swipe at Burnham's first big policy
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer's former right hand man Darren Jones says Andy Burnham's VAT cut is unfunded.

item.title: VAT to be cut from household electricity bills in October
item.description: Homes will save around £45 a year through the cut announced on Andy Burnham's first full day as prime minister.

item.title: Whitehall officials offered move to No 10 North in Manchester
item.description: London-based civil servants willing to move north have been told they will be offered relocation packages, the BBC understands.

item.title: AI minister role boosted but tech department axed in Burnham shake-up
item.description: Kanishka Narayan will attend cabinet - but no longer have the leadership of a technology secretary.

item.title: Ann Widdecombe was hit on head 21 times with hammer, prosecutors say
item.description: Joshua Kerry, 28, attacked Widdecombe while she was eating lunch at her home in Devon before stealing her wallet, prosecutors say.

item.title: Three reasons why UK electricity prices are so high
item.description: The government has announced it will cut VAT on domestic electricity to zero but Britons still pay more for power than many other European nations.

item.title: Former Labour leader's 'delight' as son becomes new Welsh secretary
item.description: Stephen Kinnock is the son of ex-Labour leader Lord Kinnock and former MEP Glenys Kinnock.

item.title: UK borrows less than expected in June but public finances remain a challenge
item.description: Public finances for last month were better than expected but the UK is carrying major debt.

item.title: Sir Chris Bryant outlines 'checklist' of issues as he takes up NI secretary role
item.description: Hilary Benn replaced by a former Anglican priest as part of a Cabinet reshuffle by the new Prime Minister, Andy Burnham.

item.title: Foreign secretary job is honour of my life, says Ed Miliband
item.description: The former energy secretary and Labour veteran was touted as a potential chancellor, but will take over from Yvette Cooper as foreign secretary.

item.title: Burnham pledges to end rough sleeping in first speech as prime minister
item.description: The Labour leader made a similar pledge as Greater Manchester mayor, although he failed to achieve this.

item.title: Burnham pledges to 'bring back hope' as he makes Healey chancellor
item.description: The new prime minister pledged to make announcements this week to "give people some breathing space".

item.title: Prisons minister Lord Timpson quits government
item.description: The businessman said it was an "honour" to serve in the post, but the BBC understands he feels he has achieved all he can in the role.

item.title: Is Burnham promising a new dawn for North Sea oil and gas?
item.description: The incoming PM is expected to announce fresh support for the North Sea from day one of his premiership.

item.title: Thames Water lenders preparing legal challenge in event of Burnham nationalisation
item.description: The BBC understands creditors would would pursue payment in full of the outstanding debts, in the multi-billions of pounds.

item.title: Digital ID plan to be scrapped in Burnham's first major policy pledge
item.description: A spokesperson for the incoming prime minister says his government would “put its focus where people need it right now”.

item.title: Sackings, cabinet surprises and policies: Burnham's first 24 hours as PM
item.description: The new prime minister enters Number 10 in a flurry of activity - but he is still facing calls for a snap election.

item.title: Who's in Andy Burnham's cabinet?
item.description: An in-depth look at which Labour MPs have been appointed to cabinet roles by new Prime Minister Andy Burnham

item.title: Louise Haigh: Labour power broker behind Burnham's rise
item.description: The Sheffield MP's return to the cabinet comes after she emerged as one of the PM's key political allies.

item.title: Angela Rayner: Cabinet comeback for one of Labour's biggest names
item.description: The potential contender to replace Keir Starmer is now back in the cabinet under Andy Burnham.

item.title: Who is new chancellor John Healey? Labour veteran who quit as defence secretary
item.description: The former defence secretary is seen as a safe pair of hands - and is now in charge of the government's finances.

item.title: Five headaches Andy Burnham will have to deal with as PM
item.description: From defence spending to housing - the next UK leader has a series of challenges to deal with.

item.title: Burnham's 'Manchesterism' got him to No 10 - but will it work for the UK?
item.description: Economics editor Faisal Islam examines whether the north-west city can be a template for the entire country.

item.title: Can Andy Burnham solve the housing crisis?
item.description: BBC Verify has looked at the scale of the housing challenge facing Burnham when he becomes PM.

item.title: Burnham set for No 10: BBC correspondents on what Trump, Putin and others will make of him
item.description: In the US, China, Europe, Russia and elsewhere, how is the UK's expected new prime minister regarded?

item.title: How a rise in ADHD benefits claims is adding to welfare spending challenge
item.description: More than 100,000 people with ADHD as their main condition receive Pip, latest figures show, an increase of 40% since Labour came to power.

item.title: How the leasehold revolution could transform neighbourly relations
item.description: The government wants to abolish leasehold, but replacing it could prove far more complicated

item.title: 'Posh George': Who is aristocrat and convicted criminal at heart of Farage controversy?
item.description: The 32-year-old reported crypto entrepreneur, once convicted of fraud in the US, is a long-time Farage ally.

item.title: Burnham's people: MPs and advisers in line for a job if he gains power
item.description: The key figures likely to be influential if Burnham succeeds in gaining the keys to Number 10.

item.title: Why Starmer's defence plan leaves next PM with £4.7bn headache
item.description: Keir Starmer announces extra defence spending - but the subject will still be one of the trickiest issues in his successor's in-tray.

item.title: Will Starmer's plan for defence help UK hit Nato's spending target?
item.description: BBC Verify looks at what the big numbers around the Defence Investment Plan mean for spending on the military and hitting the government's pledges.

item.title: Frank Gardner: Key points from government's defence spending plan
item.description: The BBC's security correspondent analyses the details of the plan, and what they mean for the UK's armed forces.

item.title: Who is Andy Burnham's wife, Marie-France van Heel?
item.description: Van Heel, known as Frankie, is a marketing executive who has been in a relationship with Burnham since their university days.

item.title: The people and places that helped Andy Burnham find self-belief
item.description: A teacher says the MP needed persuading to go to Cambridge, "but he did it and the rest is history".

item.title: Jobs, borrowing and taxes: Andy Burnham's economic challenges
item.description: The person in charge of the country will change but the financial challenges remain the same.

item.title: Ten years on, Brexit's economic impact is becoming clearer
item.description: A decade ago, many economists argued the UK would sustain longer-term economic damage by leaving the EU. So what did happen?

item.title: What we know about Andy Burnham's policies
item.description: The former Greater Manchester has begun outlining the policies he will pursue as prime minister.

item.title: Key takeaways as Andy Burnham becomes PM
item.description: The new Labour leader has formally taken office and set out his priorities for power in No 10.

item.title: Burnham has big ideas - but what will they cost?
item.description: Every tricky decision and trade off is being watched by the country and the markets at the same time

item.title: Can Burnham's casual style survive contact with No 10?
item.description: When Burnham becomes PM, he'll find it harder to wear the wardrobe that's central to his political brand.

item.title: He'll be the first Andy in No 10 - but his rise to power has been decades in the making
item.description: The outgoing Greater Manchester mayor is lining up a third attempt to be Labour leader following his return to Westminster.

item.title: Analysis: Burnham's policy choices reveal what kind of PM he wants to be
item.description: The new prime minister has begun to set out key policies - starting with a vow to end rough sleeping.

item.title: Burnham does not rule out wealth tax
item.description: The expected next PM says he may have to "ask for a little more" in tax and warns of "difficult" choices.

item.title: Row after Tory bid to make Burnham face MPs before autumn fails
item.description: The Tories have accused Labour of "running scared" after ministers denied them a chance to delay Parliament's summer break.

item.title: Cash-strapped Thames Water poses big test for Burnham
item.description: Despite returning to profit after hiking bills 40%, the deeply troubled company is far from out of the woods.

item.title: Burnham tells cabinet to tackle living costs at first meeting
item.description: The new prime minister chaired his first cabinet after announcing a cut to VAT on household electricity.

item.title: Starmer ally takes swipe at Burnham's first big policy
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer's former right hand man Darren Jones says Andy Burnham's VAT cut is unfunded.

item.title: Chris Mason: New chancellor faces tough spending choices
item.description: The appointment of John Healey is particularly fascinating, writes BBC political editor Chris Mason.

item.title: Chris Mason: Burnham is fifth PM in four years - now he'll try to buck the trend
item.description: Determined to hit the ground running, the former mayor hopes to maintain the leadership style he adopted in Manchester.

item.title: Chris Mason: Investigation into Widdecombe's death rekindles debate over how to protect politicians
item.description: Many MPs blame social media for normalising violent language and abuse, the BBC's political editor writes.

item.title: Ruthless plotter? Flip-flopper? Dad dancer? Insiders reveal the real Andy Burnham
item.description: Who is the real Andy Burnham? Laura Kuenssberg talks to more than 20 people who know him to work it out

item.title: Burnham's 'Manchesterism' got him to No 10 - but will it work for the UK?
item.description: Economics editor Faisal Islam examines whether the north-west city can be a template for the entire country.

item.title: The Nowak murder has lit a match under British politics. This is how we got here
item.description: The police response to the murder of Henry Nowak has triggered a heated transatlantic debate.

item.title: Is it harder than ever to be prime minister?
item.description: Discontent with the political class is greater than ever - has the UK become ungovernable?

item.title: Relish and dread as Starmer 'shambles' hangs over Scotland and Wales elections
item.description: Laura Kuenssberg travels to Wales and Scotland to speak to candidates and voters ahead of May 7 polls.

item.title: Bus travellers respond to fare reduction
item.description: 'It's going to help us' - public respond to bus fare cut.

item.title: 'This phase of government is about reprioritising'
item.description: Andy Burnham addresses the latest government policy announcement saying this phase of government is a reprioritising.

item.title: Transport secretary quizzed on bus fare change
item.description: Labour MP is asked about the lowering of the bus fare cap, after Starmer increased it in 2025.

item.title: How will Burnham fund £2 bus fare cap?
item.description: Most single bus fares in England will be capped at £2 from January, Prime Minister Andy Burnham announces.

item.title: Should there have been a general election?
item.description: Members of the public are asked if they think they should have being given a vote before Andy Burnham was appointed the new UK prime minister.
